title: Bridge Essay: Decoding haptic and imagined stimulus size in the human cortex.
concept: Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**Decoding haptic and imagined stimulus size in the human cortex.** - Neuroimage (2026). DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2026.121774. PMID: 41651096. Human neuroimaging studies indicate that the early visual cortex (EVC), including the primary visual cortex (V1), is involved in haptic exploration of objects, even when visual information is not available. However, it remains unknown whether the features of haptically explored objects, like size, are represented in the EVC.
